llm_result.summary: The patient reports persistent cognitive noise with racing thoughts, difficulty sustaining attention on a single task, and notable task-unrelated thinking during passive activities. Targetable levers span bio (neural/metabolic support, sleep, and exercise), psycho (CBT-based activation, mindfulness, psychoeducation), and social domains (support and belonging), plus cross-domain external cognitive aids. The proposed solutions aim to reduce internal cognitive load, improve attentional control, and re-engage with meaningful activities, while monitoring safety and feasibility. Treatments can be deployed individually or in combination, prioritizing high-impact, high-feasibility levers with clear measurement plans tied to C01–C06 criteria.

solutions.solution_candidate: Structured attention-training program using computerized tasks and real-world attention-control drills to strengthen top-down control and reduce rapid thought-splitting and cognitive noise.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Improved sustained attention and cognitive control reduces frequency and intensity of racing thoughts and task-unrelated thinking, easing subjective mental noise and distress.
solutions.measurement: Performance on sustained-attention tasks (e.g., computer-based tests); daily diary ratings of thought frequency and cognitive noise (0-10); episode-length tracking.
solutions.safety_notes: No safety concerns identified; ensure task difficulty is matched to avoid frustration.
solutions.mapping_query: Map S01 to C01-C04; track reduction in racing thoughts, attention drift, and perceived mental noise via objective tasks and daily self-reports.

solutions.solution_candidate: 8-week mindfulness program (guided or app-based) focusing on nonjudgmental awareness of thoughts to reduce reactivity and cognitive chaining.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Mindfulness reduces automatic thought elaboration and improves cognitive control, lowering intrusions and distress from mental chatter.
solutions.measurement: Weekly practice logs; Five Facet Mindfulness Questionnaire; self-reported intrusion frequency (0-10); brief cognitive-control tasks.
solutions.safety_notes: None anticipated; provide optional support if anxiety increases.
solutions.mapping_query: Link mindfulness practice to reductions in racing thoughts, attention drift, and distress; monitor with mindfulness scales and intrusion ratings.

solutions.solution_candidate: Establish consistent sleep-wake times, optimize sleep environment, and reduce screen exposure before bed to improve sleep quality and daytime cognitive control.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Better sleep enhances executive function and emotional regulation, reducing mental noise and improving engagement with tasks.
solutions.measurement: Sleep diary (bedtime, wake time, awakenings); Pittsburgh Sleep Quality Index (PSQI); daytime sleepiness scale; subjective changes in thought loudness.
solutions.safety_notes: Screen for sleep apnea or other sleep disorders; refer for evaluation if indicated.
solutions.mapping_query: Assess sleep hygiene impact on cognitive noise and thought intrusion; track via sleep metrics and PSQI.

solutions.solution_candidate: Structured behavioral activation with cognitive-behavioral techniques (thought-records, problem solving) to re-engage in activities and decrease rumination.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Increased engagement reduces idle cognitive processing and rumination, freeing cognitive resources for ongoing tasks and reducing distress from mental chatter.
solutions.measurement: Activity diary (days/week with planned activities); rumination intensity scale; time-on-task during activities; perceived engagement.
solutions.safety_notes: Monitor for initial increases in anxiety with new activities; adjust pace accordingly.
solutions.mapping_query: Link activation and CBT skills to C02-C04-C06; monitor with activity logs and rumination measures.

solutions.solution_candidate: Regular moderate aerobic activity (e.g., 30 minutes, 3–5 days/week) with gradual progression; emphasize enjoyable activities and consistency.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Exercise enhances neurotrophic factors, sleep, mood, and executive control, which supports attentional stability and reduces internal cognitive noise.
solutions.measurement: Weekly exercise minutes; step counts; self-reported cognitive noise and distraction during tasks; brief cognitive-task performance pre/post period.
solutions.safety_notes: Check medical clearance if starting new exercise; stay hydrated and listen to body cues.
solutions.mapping_query: Relate aerobic activity to improvements in attention and cognitive noise measures; monitor weekly.

solutions.solution_candidate: Scheduled social connections or group activities to provide meaningful engagement, reduce rumination, and support cognitive control through social scaffolding.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Social interaction offers distraction from internal chatter, improves mood, and provides coping resources that support attention and task engagement.
solutions.measurement: Social connectedness scale; number and quality of social interactions; perceived support; engagement duration in activities.
solutions.safety_notes: Be mindful of social overwhelm; respect boundaries and preferences.
solutions.mapping_query: Assess impact of social engagement on intrusions and engagement; track with belonging scales and interaction counts.

solutions.solution_candidate: Use digital calendars, checklists, timers, and environmental prompts to offload working memory and cue task transitions.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Externalizing cognitive load reduces internal cognitive noise and task-switching demands, promoting steadier attention and fewer intrusive thoughts.
solutions.measurement: Usage logs (apps, reminders); task completion rate; self-reported drift frequency during tasks.
solutions.safety_notes: Ensure privacy and data security; avoid over-reliance on external aids.
solutions.mapping_query: Link offloading aids to C01-C04; monitor app usage and task performance.

solutions.solution_candidate: Psychoeducational modules detailing cognitive processes, stress response, and practical self-regulation strategies; includes practice with self-monitoring and values-based actions.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Increased awareness and coping skills empower the patient to modulate thought flow and maintain engagement.
solutions.measurement: Psychoeducation engagement metrics; self-efficacy scales; pre/post cognitive-control task performance.
solutions.safety_notes: Content accessible and appropriate; provide support for comprehension as needed.
solutions.mapping_query: Link psychoeducation to cognitive control and engagement variables; track with scales and cognitive tasks.
